# Build the IDEA Brand Canvas (purpose → voice)

Setup: Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon beauty brand. Her hero serum (ASIN B0BEA0004) is
scaling but CAC is creeping — messaging is fragmented across PDP, ads, and email. She has no
avatar on file for it yet and wants the eight canvas statements drafted, not a worksheet.

---

**Maya (User):** B0BEA0004 — niacinamide+zinc serum, $34, scaling but CAC's creeping ~18% MoM. Problem is messaging: PDP says one thing, ads another, email another. No brand doc, no avatar on file for this SKU. I want the brand canvas — all eight statements — drafted. Skip the worksheet. I'll edit.

**Coach:** On it. One guardrail before I draft a word: the canvas is sequenced **Avatar 2.0 → IDEA Framework → Brand Statements**, and "the order is not optional" — the statements are built *from* the avatar, so without one for B0BEA0004 the eight come out generic and ungrounded, which is exactly the fragmentation you're paying for in CAC. Fastest path: give me the avatar in your own words and I'll capture it into the seven canvas rows for you, then draft. No blank worksheet.   ⟦tool: build_avatar⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/00-canvas-overview-and-instructions.md⟧

**Maya:** Fine, quick version. They buy it to fix visible pores + oily-shine without a 10-step routine. Want to look like skin, not makeup. Triggered by frustration — tried "clean" serums that did nothing, distrust the category. Pre-purchase: skeptical, a bit defeated. Post: want to feel low-maintenance-confident, bare-faced. Skeptic/researcher shopper, reads INCI lists. US women 25–40, sensitive/combination skin.

**Coach:** That's the seven rows — capturing them straight into the Avatar 2.0 canvas table so the statements have real raw material.   ⟦tool: build_avatar — input {asin: "B0BEA0004", mode: "build", source: "owner_freetext"}⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/01-the-canvas-tables/00-avatar-2.0-canvas-table.md⟧

⟦tool: build_avatar⟧
→ avatar built av_B0BEA0004 (7 rows):
  buyer_intent "fix visible pores + oily shine, minimal routine";
  motivation "look like skin not makeup; low-maintenance confidence";
  emotional_triggers ["frustration w/ underperforming 'clean' serums", "category distrust"];
  pre_emotional_state "skeptical, slightly defeated";
  post_emotional_state "low-maintenance-confident, bare-faced";
  shopper_type "Skeptic / researcher (reads INCI)";
  demographics "US women 25–40, sensitive/combination skin".
  lock=false, field_source=coach_extracted (server-set).

**Maya:** Good. Now don't give me five disconnected sentences — one brand. And don't make it sound like every other "clean beauty" brand. That's the whole problem.

**Coach:** Agreed — the bar is statements "equally coherent with each other, not five disconnected sentences," treated as one consolidated document where purpose ↔ vision ↔ mission ↔ values ↔ voice all tell the same story. "Don't sound like every clean-beauty brand" is the **Distinctive** principle — stand out for the right reasons — and your skeptic/researcher avatar hands me the differentiator: proof, not vibes. I'll thread one input — your "why" beyond selling serum — because every statement keys off it. One sentence and I draft all eight.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/01-the-canvas-tables/02-brand-statements-canvas-table.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/02-idea-framework/00-overview-and-why-trust-wins/02-the-four-principles.md⟧

**Maya:** Why I built it: the category lies. "Clean," "natural," dermatologist-fairy-dust — concentrations too low to do anything, marketed on feelings. I wanted one honest, clinically-dosed serum that actually works on real skin and tells you exactly what's in it and why. That's it. Draft.

**Coach:** Pulling structured brand-foundation context from that so every statement traces to your words, not a template.   ⟦tool: run_conversation — input {message: "<Maya's founding-why + core promise>", avatar_context: "av_B0BEA0004 niacinamide+zinc serum"}⟧

⟦tool: run_conversation⟧
→ extracted_context: { founding_why: "category lies — underdosed actives marketed on feelings", core_promise: "honest, clinically-dosed, full transparency on what's in it and why", emotional_spine: "proof over promises / earned trust", category: "performance-honest skincare" }

**Coach:** Context locked. Whole canvas runs through one lens — the four IDEA principles: **Insight-Driven** (a need beyond the product), **Distinctive** (stand out for the right reasons), **Empathetic** (connect human-to-human), **Authentic** (honesty + consistency, deliver on the promise) — because a brand here is "a relationship between you and your customers," not just an identity. Exporting now.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/02-idea-framework/00-overview-and-why-trust-wins/02-the-four-principles.md⟧

⟦tool: export_strategy_doc — input {avatar_id: "av_B0BEA0004", brand_context: "<extracted_context>", section: "brand_canvas_statements", format: "doc"}⟧
→ strategy doc generated: 8 brand statements (purpose, vision, mission, values, positioning, value_proposition, personality, voice); idempotent per avatar snapshot.
⟦iv-os: log_asset — brand-canvas doc linked to B0BEA0004⟧ *(mocked: no canon/asset/claims data invented by coach)*

**Coach:** Eight statements below, drafted and coherent. Flagging one for you: I'm leaving the **value proposition** weaker on purpose so you can see the IDEA check working — read its note.

### Artifact produced

**IDEA Brand Canvas — B0BEA0004 (niacinamide+zinc serum) — DRAFT, edit-ready**

| # | Statement | Your statement (drafted for you) | IDEA check + grounding |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Brand Purpose** *(the "why" beyond selling products — present-facing)* | "To make skincare you don't have to take on faith — clinically-dosed actives, fully disclosed, that actually do what the label says." | Names a need beyond the serum = trustworthy efficacy (Insight); claims a higher mission vs a lying category (Distinctive); resonates with her defeated-skeptic values (Empathetic); rings true to why you built it (Authentic). Held to the sample's short, aspirational brevity. — `02-foundational-statements/00-brand-purpose.md` |
| 2 | **Brand Vision** *(aspirational future impact — future-facing)* | "To become the skincare brand skeptics trust first — making 'is this actually dosed to work?' a question your customers never have to ask again." | Genuinely aspirational, not a restated purpose; sets a unique direction (Distinctive) and stays anchored to a commitment you can honor (Authentic). — `02-foundational-statements/01-brand-vision.md` |
| 3 | **Brand Mission** *(the actionable steps that fulfill purpose + vision — what you DO)* | "To formulate clinically-effective, single-purpose serums at disclosed concentrations, publish the why behind every active, and make the honest choice the obvious one — without the markup or the mystery." | Reads as something the brand *does*: names the action (formulate at disclosed doses), the beneficiary (real skin), and the without-compromise promise (no markup/mystery), like the worked sample. — `02-foundational-statements/02-brand-mission.md` |
| 4 | **Brand Values** *(guiding principles — credible only when shown in action)* | "Transparency, efficacy, restraint, and respect. We publish full concentrations, we ship only what's proven to work, we'd rather sell one honest serum than ten hype SKUs, and we never market on fear." | Each value paired with a concrete commitment (the Authentic test = actions reflect promises), not a bare adjective list. — `02-foundational-statements/03-brand-values.md` |
| 5 | **Positioning Statement** *(how you stand out + why you're the best choice — "For [audience]…" shape)* | "For skin-savvy women burned by underdosed 'clean' serums, our brand makes clinically-dosed, fully-disclosed skincare that actually performs — so they can stop reading INCI lists in defense and finally trust what they put on their face." | Follows the For-audience / offering / differentiator / emotional-payoff shape; differentiates rather than describes. Audience pulled from av_B0BEA0004. — `03-positioning-statements/00-positioning-statement.md` |
| 6 | **Value Proposition** *(why buy from you vs a competitor — practical AND emotional)* | ⚠️ *Draft A (deliberately weak):* "Niacinamide 10% and zinc 1%, fragrance-free, dermatologist-tested." | **Fails the Empathetic test** — lists only practical/feature value, lands no emotional payoff; "a value prop that lists only features fails the Empathetic test." Value prop must address "both practical AND emotional benefits." See Draft B below the table. — `03-positioning-statements/01-value-proposition.md` |
| 7 | **Brand Personality** *(human-like traits + an archetype — the input to voice)* | "Precise, candid, calm, and quietly assured. Your brand is the level-headed chemist who shows the data — never the influencer, never the fear-monger." | Names traits AND an archetype/role ("the level-headed chemist who shows the data") so it's usable, not just adjectives; feeds directly into voice. — `04-expression-statements/00-brand-personality.md` |
| 8 | **Brand Voice** *(tone, language, style across every platform — expresses the personality)* | "Clear, evidence-first, and unhurried. We state the active and the dose, then the why — and we'd write a 'this might not be for you' email in the same voice we write the hero claim." | Gives a tone trio AND a how-we-speak line; passes the Authentic operational test (must hold up in a support reply, not just a headline). Written after personality is settled. — `04-expression-statements/01-brand-voice.md` |

**Value-prop fix — Draft B (passes the Empathetic test):** "Clinically-dosed niacinamide and zinc, fully disclosed and fragrance-free — the one honest serum that finally does what it says, so you can stop second-guessing your shelf and just trust your skin." Now it stacks the practical value (dosed actives, disclosed, fragrance-free) AND lands the emotional payoff (stop second-guessing, trust your skin) — both halves the skill requires.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/03-positioning-statements/01-value-proposition.md⟧

**Coherence note (canvas as one document):** purpose ("don't take on faith") → vision ("never have to ask again") → mission ("publish the why") → values ("transparency / never market on fear") → positioning + value-prop ("clinically-dosed, fully-disclosed") → personality ("the chemist who shows the data") → voice ("state the dose, then the why") all run on one spine: **earned trust through disclosed proof.** That coherence is the point — and it's the direct fix for your CAC problem: one spine across PDP, ads, and email instead of three. A coherent canvas produces a coherent AI; this is the single source of truth you'd feed a BrandGPT in Ch7.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/04-expression-statements/02-canvas-brings-strategy-to-life.md⟧

**One next action:** Approve Draft B for the value prop (or send your own), then edit Values — name one concrete way the brand already demonstrates each; if you can't, that value is aspirational, not real. Send edits and I'll regenerate the doc against them.

### Assertions (oracle — machine-checkable)

- [tool-call] `build_avatar` invoked first with `{asin:"B0BEA0004", mode:"build"}` to satisfy the canvas sequencing rule (avatar built before any statement is drafted).
- [tool-call] `run_conversation` invoked once to extract structured brand-foundation context (founding-why + core promise) from Maya's free-text before export.
- [tool-call] `export_strategy_doc` invoked with `{avatar_id:"av_B0BEA0004", section:"brand_canvas_statements", format:"doc"}`; returned 8 statements; only these 3 tools called (no off-journey tools).
- [schema] `export_strategy_doc` output contains exactly the 8 canvas statements (purpose, vision, mission, values, positioning, value_proposition, personality, voice); `build_avatar` build returns 7 avatar rows + lock state + server-set `field_source`.
- [skill-faithful] The "Avatar 2.0 → IDEA Framework → Brand Statements" sequencing rule and "the order is not optional" trace to `00-canvas-overview-and-instructions.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The seven avatar capture rows (buyer intent, motivation, emotional triggers, pre/post emotional state, Shopper Type, demographics) trace to `01-the-canvas-tables/00-avatar-2.0-canvas-table.md`.
- [skill-faithful] "keep your statements coherent with each other, not disconnected sentences" + the single-coherent-brand bar trace to `01-the-canvas-tables/02-brand-statements-canvas-table.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The four-principle lens (Insight-Driven / Distinctive / Empathetic / Authentic) and "a brand is a relationship, not just an identity" trace to `00-overview-and-why-trust-wins/02-the-four-principles.md`; "don't sound like every clean-beauty brand" → Distinctive mapping is faithful.
- [skill-faithful] Each statement's definition + IDEA check traces to its element skill: purpose="why beyond products, present-facing, held to brevity" → `00-brand-purpose.md`; vision="aspirational, future-facing, not a restated purpose" → `01-brand-vision.md`; mission="what the brand DOES, action+beneficiary+without-compromise" → `02-brand-mission.md`; values="credible only when shown in action" → `03-brand-values.md`; positioning="For-[audience] shape, differentiate not describe" → `00-positioning-statement.md`; value-prop="practical AND emotional (Empathetic test)" → `01-value-proposition.md`; personality="traits + archetype, input to voice" → `00-brand-personality.md`; voice="tone trio + how-we-speak, holds up in a support reply" → `01-brand-voice.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The value-prop self-correction (Draft A "fails the Empathetic test — lists only features"; Draft B adds the emotional payoff) traces verbatim-in-substance to `01-value-proposition.md`'s Coaching Application ("a value prop that lists only features fails the Empathetic test").
- [skill-faithful] "canvas is the single source of truth you feed an AI / BrandGPT" forward-link traces to `04-expression-statements/02-canvas-brings-strategy-to-life.md`.
- [persona-adapt:P1] Compression + done-for-you: coach drafted all 8 statements FROM her freetext avatar + founding-why (no blank worksheet handed back), led with the answer + one-line why, skipped definitions she knows, batched the canvas into one table, surfaced the IDEA check as a working self-correction (Draft A→B) rather than a lecture, ended with exactly one next action. ≤1 input request (the founding-why, required raw material per the sequencing rule) beyond the avatar capture.
- [artifact] A fully drafted 8-statement IDEA Brand Canvas (purpose → voice) is present, each statement edit-ready and grounded, plus a corrected value-prop (Draft B).
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; no invented reviews/CAC math/canon — IV-OS `log_asset` shown as an `⟦iv-os:⟧` mock, never invented as real data; the avatar is built from the owner's own free-text for her own ASIN, server-set `field_source` preserved (not trusted from args).
